title: Software Engineer, Commerce Backend
description:
**Company Overview**
 Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1\\.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business\\-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the \\#1 company in e\\-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM).
   

  

 What you'll do
   

  

 Docusign is looking for a highly motivated full stack Software Engineer to join our commerce platform team. This person will work in a close knitted development Pod of 4\\-6 engineers to develop, test, and support features and experimentation on ecom.docusign.com and adjacent experiences.
   

  

 The Commerce engineering team offers unique learning opportunities where you will be directly involved in end\\-to\\-end customer experience ownership across a large surface area of product and engineering collaboration. We look for candidates with love for computer science and engineering, and we truly believe you will enjoy and find success in this team.
   

  

 This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Engineering Manager, Commerce Platform.
   

  

**Responsibility**
* Be open minded, forward thinking and passionate about great software
* Think about how to solve problems in scale and build fault tolerant systems
* Develop testable, high\\-quality, and ship\\-ready code with ample test coverage
* Work with Product Management and other stakeholders to understand and translate commerce and growth teams, and build end to end architecture and system designs
* Lead the end to end architecture and systems designs by partnering with cross functional teams for the PLG org
* Mentor and address other senior and lead engineers and architects within the organization
* Represent PLG engineering org in all discussions with the stakeholders
* Identify and build POCs to solve technical challenges within the organization and recommend a scalable solution
* Experience presenting software architecture to the executives
* Work as part of a cross\\-site development team to drive the design, implementation, testing, and release of products


 Job Designation
   

  

**Hybrid:** 
 Employee divides their time between in\\-office and remote work. Access to an office location is required. (Frequency: Minimum 2 days per week; may vary by team but will be weekly in\\-office expectation)
   

  

 Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law.
   

  

 What you bring
   

  

**Basic**
* Bachelor's or Master’s in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Information Systems, Informatics or equivalent experience
* 5\\+ years of software development experience in user\\-facing applications
* Experience working with C\\#, Go, Java, React, Java Script, or other modern programming languages
* Experience using coding assistants \\- Github Copilot, Claude Code etc.


**Preferred**
* Experience in backend development
* Experienced building or consuming RESTful web\\-services
* Experience in distributed systems
* Involvement in the design and operation of large\\-scale cloud services
* Experiences with the entire software development lifecycle, including version control (git), build process, testing, and code release
* Strong organizational, problem\\-solving and communication skills
* Ability to work in a dynamic, fast\\-moving environment, prioritize your work and manage your own time
* Ability and desire to move across technology stacks fluently and easily
* Passion for working on a highly available site
* Passion to learn new software technologies


 Life at Docusign
   

  

**Working here**
 Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal.

title: QA Engineer (Standard)
description:
**Roles \\& Responsibilities**
 Manual QA Tester who will be responsible for ensuring that Kia America website function smoothly and accurately. The role involves manually checking website availability, validating UI elements, verifying backend data accuracy,
   

  

 and testing promotional offers and incentive configurations.
   

  

 You will work closely with AEM\\-based web applications, test new enhancements and maintenance tickets, compare incentive data against legal documentation, and report any issues through structured defect tracking.
   

  

 The goal is to ensure every release meets quality standards before going live.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

* Perform manual testing to ensure websites are live, accessible, and functioning as expected.
* Validate UI components, layout, responsiveness, and user workflows across environments.
* Identify, document, and report defects with clear reproduction steps.
* Test and validate maintenance tickets and enhancements deployed on AEM.
* Compare incentive configurations against legal/business documents to ensure data accuracy and compliance.
* Update and manage JIRA tickets with detailed test results, comments, and status updates.
* Collaborate with developers to clarify requirements and resolve defects.
* Perform regression testing to ensure existing functionalities are unaffected by new releases.


**Required Skills \\& Qualifications**
* Experience in Manual QA / Website Testing.
* Basic understanding of Adobe Experience Manager (AEM).
* Strong knowledge of UI validation and functional testing.
* Experience using JIRA or similar defect tracking tools.
* Strong attention to detail and analytical thinking.
* Good communication and documentation skills.


**Preferred Skills**
* Understanding of web technologies (HTML, CSS, basic JavaScript).
* Knowledge of regression and UAT testing processes.


**And Work Timings**
 3:30pm IST to 12:30am IST \\+\\- 1hr considering the day light savings
   

  

 Monday : Can be a night shift if incase there's an issue with tickets on QA, at that point we will have to wait for developers fix.
   

  

 Tuesday: Can be a night shift if incase there's an issue with tickets on STG, at that point we will have to wait for developers fix.
   

  

 Wednesday : Definitely Night shift as Deployement to Prod will take place.
   

  

 Thursday and Friday : No night shifts, but again if some work comes up during SCRUM call, we will have to support that.
   

  

 Every 1st week of the Month we will get Incentives which we will have to test within the same day(sometimes goes on to next day if we get very late). So night shift for sure, maybe working till 7\\-9am IST too.
   

  

**Experience**
* 4\\.5\\-6 Years

* Sub Skill(s): Manual Testing
* Additional Skill(s): Mobile Testing, QAComplete, Manual Testing

title: Forward Deployed engineer
description:
**About GreyLabs AI** 




 GreyLabs AI is building the voice operating system for India's BFSI. Our Agentic Voice AI platform helps banks, insurers, NBFCs, and fintechs automate and humanize millions of customer conversations \\- across sales, collections, customer service, and compliance \\- in multiple Indian languages.
 



  





 In under two years, we've scaled to 50\\+ enterprise clients including RBL Bank, AU Small Finance Bank, IDFC FIRST Bank, SBI Life, ICICI Prudential Life, Motilal Oswal, and Groww \\- processing hundreds of millions of conversations. We raised ₹85 Crores in Series A funding led by Elevation Capital with Z47, and were recognized for "Best Use of AI in Fintech" at IFTA 2025\\.
 



  





  





**The Role** 




 We're hiring a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) to work at the intersection of engineering and customer delivery \\- translating enterprise business problems into production AI deployments on the GreyLabs platform.
 



  





 This is not a back\\-office engineering role. You'll sit across the table from banking ops leads, product managers at insurance companies, and tech teams at fintechs \\- understanding their workflows, designing AI\\-powered solutions, building integrations, and owning the path from pilot to production. You'll be the person who makes GreyLabs work in the real world, across real systems, for real users.
 



  





 One week you might be designing a multilingual collections voice agent for a major bank, configuring conversation flows, and integrating with their CRM. The next, you could be debugging a data pipeline issue on a live deployment, optimizing containment rates based on production conversation logs, or running a technical workshop for a client's engineering team.
 



  





 This role demands equal parts technical depth and customer instinct. You'll write code, build integrations, configure platforms, and troubleshoot production systems \\- but you'll also need to listen well, communicate clearly, and develop a genuine understanding of how financial institutions operate. If you've ever felt that the best engineering happens closest to the customer, this role was built for you.
 



  





  





**What You'll Do** 




  




* **Own enterprise implementations end\\-to\\-end** 
 \\- from understanding business workflows and designing AI use\\-cases, to configuring the platform, building integrations, driving pilots, and ensuring successful production rollout
* **Build and configure AI workflows** 
 on the GreyLabs platform \\- prompts, conversation logic, routing rules, fallback handling, and automation behavior \\- customized by industry and use\\-case (collections, customer service, sales, compliance)
* **Integrate with enterprise systems** 
 \\- CRMs, ticketing tools, dialers, internal APIs, and databases using Python, REST APIs, webhooks, SFTP/S3, and batch data pipelines
* **Be the technical face of GreyLabs** 
 during implementation \\- run workshops, demos, and troubleshooting sessions with client engineering and ops teams; debug production issues and drive resolution
* **Improve outcomes continuously** 
 \\- analyze conversation logs, system behavior, and performance metrics to optimize accuracy, containment, and operational efficiency across deployments
* **Feed insights back to product** 
 \\- identify patterns across customer deployments that should become platform features, reusable templates, or accelerators for future implementations



  





  





**What We're Looking For** 




  





**Must\\-Haves** 



* 3 \\- 5 years of professional experience in a technical role \\- software engineering, solutions engineering, technical consulting, or implementation engineering
* Strong Python skills \\- you're comfortable writing scripts, building API integrations, and handling data transformations in production contexts
* Working experience with REST APIs, webhooks, and authentication patterns (OAuth, token\\-based auth)
* Familiarity with data connectivity \\- SFTP, S3, or batch data ingestion workflows
* Ability to debug real production issues \\- you can read logs, trace data flows, and figure out what's broken without someone handing you the answer
* Strong communication skills \\- you can translate between engineering and business teams, explain technical concepts to non\\-technical stakeholders, and hold your own in a room with enterprise clients
* An ownership mindset \\- you care about customer outcomes, not just task completion; you'll follow a problem through to resolution even when it crosses team boundaries



  





**Strong Signals (not checkboxes)** 



* Experience in a client\\-facing technical role \\- Solutions Engineer, Implementation Engineer, FDE, or Technical Consultant at a SaaS or consulting company
* Exposure to conversational AI, voice platforms, workflow automation, or LLM\\-based systems
* Understanding of CRM, contact center, collections, or customer support systems \\- especially in BFSI
* Familiarity with prompt engineering or configuring AI agent behavior
* Cloud fundamentals (AWS or GCP) \\- enough to navigate infrastructure and deployment
* Ability to quickly learn new domains \\- BFSI has its own language and complexity, and we'll bring you up to speed, but a fast learning curve helps



  





  





**Why GreyLabs AI** 



* **Work where AI meets the real world:** 
 This isn't research or prototyping \\- you'll deploy AI systems that handle millions of conversations for India's largest financial institutions. The feedback loop between what you build and how it performs is immediate.
* **Fastest career development path available:** 
 FDEs at early\\-stage companies develop breadth that takes years to accumulate elsewhere. You'll build product intuition, customer empathy, technical depth, and business understanding simultaneously \\- the foundation for Solutions Architect, Product, or Customer Engineering leadership roles.
* **Direct exposure to enterprise AI at scale:** 
 You'll work with 50\\+ enterprise clients across banking, insurance, and fintech \\- each with different systems, workflows, and constraints. This is a masterclass in building AI that works in production, not just in demos.
* **Ownership and impact from day one:** 
 At our stage, every FDE directly influences customer outcomes and product direction. Your field insights will shape what we build next.
* **Strong backing, proven team:** 
 Elevation Capital and Z47 are long\\-term partners invested in our vision. Our founders built and exited Cogno AI \\- they understand what it takes to build AI companies that earn enterprise trust.

 As an Product Quality Specialist you will be a team member in our cross\\-functional SCRUM team in India. You will be responsible for ensuring product quality

* eCATT knowledge would be an advantage.

This is an environment where you can have it all \\- the agility, enthusiasm and dynamism of a start\\-up, combined with the established expertise, solid market presence and extensive customer network of a more established organisation.
   

  

 At SAP Fioneer, you will benefit from a flexible work environment that encourages creativity and encourages you to think outside the box, bring new ideas to the table, and challenge the status quo. You will become part of a diverse and global team that we are proud of and are constantly growing and reinventing. As an employee, you will have the chance to chart your own career path and take advantage of competitive compensation packages and progression opportunities based on merit.

Additionally, our mobility benefits vary based on seniority level and include company car benefits and transportation assistance. Furthermore, we provide additional perks such as celebration rewards, a jubilee program, and a meal program.
   

  

 SAP FIONEER DIVERSITY COMMITMENT
   

  

 SAP Fioneer believes in the power of innovation that each employee brings and would like to leverage the qualities and appreciate the unique competencies that each diverse person brings to the company.

title: Golang Developer
description:
Job Description:
 



 We're building a team that owns production incident response, deep debugging, and permanent fixes across application, data, and deployment layers.
 



 This is not a tickets\\-only ops role. You will write code, ship fixes safely, and harden the platform so issues don't repeat.
 



 Note: This is a SRE/software engineering role with real production ownership. You’ll combine engineering and operations to own outcomes end\\-to\\-end: investigate incidents, ship code fixes, and prevent repeat issues through tests, observability, and hardening.
 



 What you'll do
 



 ·     Lead and execute production incident response: triage, mitigation, stakeholder communication, and coordination across teams
 



 ·     Debug and fix issues across Go services (mandatory) and the broader stack (Node.js services where relevant)
 



 ·     Work across service boundaries: GraphQL/RPC, distributed tracing, dependency failures, performance bottlenecks, and safe degradation patterns
 



 ·     Troubleshoot Kubernetes workloads and deployments
 



 ·     Diagnose PostgreSQL/CNPG issues
 



 ·     Handle production bugs that span application \\+ data pipelines (ETL/Snowflake mappings), including backfills/replays and data\\-quality validation
 



 ·     Build prevention: add regression tests, improve observability , and maintain runbooks/service passports
 



 ·     Drive reliability improvements: SLOs/SLIs, alert quality, release readiness checks, and operational standards across teams
 



 What we're looking for (must\\-have)
 



 ·     4 years in SRE / Production Engineering / Platform Engineering (reliability\\-focused)
 



 ·     Strong Go (mandatory): ability to read, debug, and ship production fixes in Go codebases
 



 ·     Proven experience debugging distributed systems in production (latency, error rates, timeouts, retries, cascading failures)
 



 ·     Strong hands\\-on experience with Kubernetes in production environments
 



 ·     Experience with Helm and GitOps workflows (FluxCD preferred; ArgoCD acceptable)
 



 ·     Solid PostgreSQL troubleshooting experience (performance, incident patterns, migrations)
 



 ·     Observability experience (metrics/logging/tracing; Datadog/Grafana/Tempo/Loki experience is a plus)
 



 ·     Strong incident leadership: calm under pressure, clear communication, structured problem\\-solving
 



 ·     Engineering hygiene: PR discipline, reviews, testing mindset, safe rollouts/rollbacks
 



 ·     Comfortable with IAM/security fundamentals in real production systems: OAuth2/OIDC basics, RBAC/least privilege, and safe secrets handling
 



 Nice\\-to\\-have
 



 ·     Node.js backend experience in production
 



 ·     Experience in FinTech / regulated environments / high\\-availability systems (auditability, change control, incident rigor)
 



 ·     Data reliability experience: ETL monitoring, reconciliation, Snowflake operations, schema/mapping drift handling
 



 ·     Reliability patterns common to trading/fintech platforms: correctness and data integrity mindset (idempotency, reconciliation), resilient partner integrations, and strong observability for critical user journeys
 



 Why join
 



 ·     Build a new function with real impact on reliability and engineering culture
 



 ·     Work across the full production surface area: application \\+ platform \\+ database \\+ data pipelines \\+ Our Whole Product
 



 ·     High ownership role: you'll influence production standards, tooling, and release safety across teams
 



  





 If interested please share CV's to \\-
 **himapriya.gunukula@speridian.com**

description:
Flexing It is a freelance consulting marketplace that connects freelancers and independent consultants with organisations seeking independent talent.
 



  





 Flexing It has partnered with Our client, a leading mine service solutions partner is looking for a Senior OutSystems Developer. This role reports to the Business Improvement Manager and will be working closely with business leaders to embed innovative digital solutions. You will work on high\\-impact projects transforming current processes and promoting data\\-driven decision\\-making. You must exhibit strong initiative, excellent analytical and problem\\-solving skills, and the ability to work autonomously.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Lead the full OutSystems development lifecycle: solution design, architecture decisions, component development, integrations, testing, deployment, documentation, and ongoing optimization.
* Design AI\\-powered features within OutSystems applications using OutSystems' native AI capabilities like OutSystems AI Mentor Studio and external AI services (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock).
* Mentor developers to take increasing responsibilities and get certified in OutSystems. Enforce strict adherence to OutSystems best practices, code reviews and performance standards.
* Translate business requirements into scalable OutSystems applications by producing technical specifications, data models, UI/UX flows, logic diagrams,and performance\\-focused designs.
* Build, enhance, and maintain OutSystems applications with clean architecture, reusable components.
* Own integrations with external systems (REST/SOAP APIs, SQL databases, authentication services, document repositories, and enterprise tools) and ensure they are secure, reliable, and monitored.
* Migrate legacy systems or manual processes into OutSystems applications through structured discovery, solution design, build, UAT coordination, and production rollout.
* Work with cross\\-functional teams. i.e., Business, Data, BA, IT to prioritize enhancements, resolve blockers, and deliver high\\-quality releases on time.
* Define and execute a structured application change\\-management approach: versioning, branching, deployment pipelines, rollback planning, dependency analysis, and release notes.
* Perform independent technical assessments to identify platform limitations, performance bottlenecks, architectural risks, and opportunities for automation or re\\-usability.
* Collaborate with the Data \\& Analytics team to ensure application data structures, APIs, and event flows support reporting, dashboards, and long\\-term analytics needs.
* Manage assigned development tasks using project management tools like ClickUp, establish and follow agile Delivery (backlog refinement, sprint planning, demos, retros).



  





**Note:** 



* This project is for 2\\-4months.

 Skills Required

* Strong understanding of OutSystems best practices: performance patterns, lifetime deployment, error handling frameworks, security roles, reusable UI patterns, and asynchronous processes.

title: Senior Back End Engineer
description:
**This is a foundational engineering role.** 
 Not a role where you just optimize endpoints or manage tickets. You’ll be helping architect the backend of a global, privacy\\-first communication platform— built from the ground up, on principles we don’t compromise. It’s an opportunity to craft a product that users love—and one that holds its own against WhatsApp, Teams, or Signal, not just in UX, but in security, auditability, and data control. You’ll build systems that deliver consumer\\-grade performance and military\\-grade privacy. We’re not iterating on someone else’s blueprint. We’re carving a new one.
 



  





**Who are we?** 




  





 We are a young product company scaling fast and outgrowing the startup tag—with global scale in our crosshairs. We don’t do “okay.” We give everything we’ve got to build the best communication platform in the world. Our team includes engineers from IIT and other top institutions—people who care deeply about the craft, performance, and purpose. You’ll learn more in 6 months here than most engineers do in 3 years elsewhere. We’re intense. High\\-strung. Focused. Our team includes engineers from IIT and other top\\-tier
 



 backgrounds. But what defines us is not our degrees—it’s our refusal to compromise on quality, performance, or privacy.
 



  





**What are we building?** 




  





 WrkTalk is the world's first zero\\-access, zero\\-spam enterprise communication product that operates on a structural privacy architecture. One that’s already earned the trust of large enterprises like L\\&T Finance, Adani Group, and Kalyan Jewellers – just to name a few of our enterprise clients. This is not an MVP. This is production\\-grade software that secures communication for thousands—and soon, millions—of professionals worldwide.
 



  





**What’s the role?** 




  





 • Design and build blazing\\-fast, secure APIs using NestJS and Golang
 



 • Architect robust microservices that scale and perform under pressure
 



 • Engineer real\\-time communication using WebSockets, WebRTC, and mediasoup
 



 • Optimize and tune PostgreSQL and Redis for lightning\\-fast access and reliability
 



 • Lead Dockerized deployments and manage Kubernetes clusters
 



 • Collaborate with our high\\-performance team of frontend devs, DevOps, and product leaders to ship elite\\-quality code
 



 • Build for zero\\-trust environments—because no data should ever leak
 



  





**The tech here is serious.** 




 We work on socket\\-driven comms infrastructure, distributed control towers, multi\\-category encryption, root detection, app kill\\-switches, dynamic guest access, device\\-level lockout, and OOO/ DND logic layered with smart whitelisting. This is not lightweight app dev. This is protocol\\-layer decision\\-making.
 



  





**Why should you join?** 




  





 • Because you want to own backend architecture—not just follow it
 



 • Because you’ll work directly with established founders, not buried under layers
 



 • Because you’ll ship real, deeply engineered features used by large number of enterprise users
 



 • Because you’ll grow 10x faster here—in experience, learning, and impact
 



 • And because if you walk this road with us for 3–4 years, it’ll catapult you into a tech orbit or fame and glory you never imagined
 



  





**Who should apply?** 




  





 • 5\\+ years of backend experience building high\\-performance systems
 



 • Expertise in Golang and Node.js (NestJS)
 



 • Deep knowledge of PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, Kubernetes
 



 • Strong command over microservices, RESTful APIs, and authentication protocols
 



 • Prior experience with real\\-time systems (WebSockets, WebRTC, mediasoup is a big plus)
 



 • Builders. Owners. With hunger to ship fast, fail fast, and optimize endlessly
 



  





**Where are we located?** 




 We’re based in Navi Mumbai and building for the world.
